data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260510T120403-5c226dd9/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260510T120403-5c226dd9/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260510T120403-5c226dd9/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260510T120403-5c226dd9/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260510T120403-5c226dd9/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-2257460f">
    <title>fix(agent/land): SyncWorkTreeToHead clobbers .ddx/beads.jsonl, wiping live state</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  After a successful bead land, the post-land
  syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths step overwrites the live
  .ddx/beads.jsonl with the version committed in the landed worktree.
  That committed version is a snapshot from when the agent claimed
  the bead — not the authoritative current state. Any operator
  writes to beads.jsonl that happened after the claim (e.g.,
  `ddx bead update <id> --set queue-rank=N`, dep edits, priority
  bumps) are silently reverted on the next land.

  Observed: I set queue-rank on ~12 beads via `ddx bead update --set
  queue-rank=N`. Later, after the drain landed unrelated beads, all
  queue-rank values had vanished (parseQueueRank returned empty).
  The drain reverted to default sort (priority + creation date),
  ignoring my prioritization.

  This is a silent data loss. No error is surfaced. The operator's
  intended ordering simply stops working after the next land.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:384 RealLandingGitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead
    delegates to syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:429
    syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths runs `git checkout-index -f` for
    every file in the fromRev..HEAD diff, except those listed in
    skipPaths (today: only files dirty-before-land are skipped via
    dirtyBefore guard).
  - .ddx/beads.jsonl is committed inside the landing worktree as
    part of agent evidence (claim, heartbeat, close events). It
    therefore appears in the fromRev..HEAD diff at land time.
  - The dirty-file guard at land time does NOT save it: if the
    operator's `--set` write was already committed via
    beadAutoCommit (which the agent itself runs to checkpoint
    tracker state), the file appears "clean" at land time → not
    skipped → got overwritten with the older landed version.
  - Same bug applies to .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl by the same
    mechanism.

  No code path in cli/internal/bead/ explicitly clears queue-rank;
  the marshal round-trip preserves unknown Extra keys correctly.
  This is purely a git-overwrite issue at the agent/land seam.

PROPOSED FIX
  Treat .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl as live-state
  files managed by the bead.Store lock — never authoritative from a
  landed commit. They must be UNCONDITIONALLY skipped by
  SyncWorkTreeToHead, regardless of dirty-before-land state.

  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go RealLandingGitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead
    (line 384 area): change call signature to pass an unconditional
    skip list:
        return syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths(dir, fromRev, []string{
            ".ddx/beads.jsonl",
            ".ddx/beads-archive.jsonl",
        })
    Or: introduce a new constant
        var alwaysSkipSyncPaths = []string{".ddx/beads.jsonl", ".ddx/beads-archive.jsonl"}
    and merge with the existing dirtyBefore set inside
    syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths.
  - Document at the call site why these paths are special: they are
    governed by bead.Store WithLock; the committed worktree version
    is a stale snapshot.
  - Add the regression test described in AC.

  ADDITIONALLY, add comprehensive lifecycle preservation tests for
  queue-rank to cover the full set of transitions that operators
  expect to be safe (zero tests exist today for this).

NON-SCOPE
  - Refactoring SyncWorkTreeToHead beyond the always-skip list.
  - Other "live state" files in .ddx/ that might have similar
    issues (executions/, attachments/) — those are append-only or
    per-attempt and don't share the snapshot problem; only the
    JSONL stores do.
  - The network-free Land() refactor (sister bead ddx-1f774edf will
    rewrite this file; this fix should land first or be merged into
    that work).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead ddx-1f774edf (network-free Land code) will refactor
    execute_bead_land.go heavily. This bead's fix should land first
    so subsequent drain prioritization is reliable; otherwise
    ddx-1f774edf's own queue-rank may be wiped before it gets
    picked up.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go RealLandingGitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead unconditionally skips .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl regardless of dirty-before-land state.
2. TestSyncWorkTreeToHead_DoesNotClobberBeadsJSONL in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go: stages a beads.jsonl change after a landing commit was created, runs SyncWorkTreeToHead, asserts the live file is unchanged (the landed snapshot does NOT overwrite it).
3. TestSyncWorkTreeToHead_DoesNotClobberBeadsArchiveJSONL: same shape, for the archive file.
4. TestQueueRank_PreservedAcrossClaim in cli/internal/bead/store_test.go (or queue_lifecycle_test.go): set queue-rank=5, claim the bead, read back, assert queue-rank still 5.
5. TestQueueRank_PreservedAcrossUnclaim: same shape, claim then unclaim.
6. TestQueueRank_PreservedAcrossClose: set queue-rank, close via CloseWithEvidence, read back, assert preserved.
7. TestQueueRank_PreservedAcrossReopen: set queue-rank, close, reopen, assert preserved.
8. TestQueueRank_PreservedAcrossReconcile: set queue-rank, run ReconcileLifecycleMetadata, assert preserved.
9. TestQueueRank_PreservedAcrossMigrateLifecycle: set queue-rank, run MigrateLifecycle, assert preserved.
10. TestQueueRank_PreservedAcrossArchive: set queue-rank, archive via ArchiveWithEvents, read from archive, assert preserved.
11. TestQueueRank_RoundTripViaJSONL: write a bead with queue-rank to JSONL, read back, assert parseQueueRank succeeds.
12. TestQueueRank_SetFlagStringRoundTrip: simulate `ddx bead update --set queue-rank=5` (which stores as string), persist, reload, assert parseQueueRank returns 5.
13. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/... green.
14. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:agent, area:bead, kind:fix, observed-failure, prevention</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="2e73da91ae7e5bf406536cc36b14bd14bb2d54a9" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260510T120403-5c226dd9"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All 3 files are correctly committed. Every AC is satisfied:

- **AC1**: `alwaysSkipSyncPaths` unconditionally skips `.ddx/beads.jsonl` and `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl` in `SyncWorkTreeToHead` (`execute_bead_land.go:384`)
- **AC2**: `TestSyncWorkTreeToHead_DoesNotClobberBeadsJSONL` in `execute_bead_land_test.go`
- **AC3**: `TestSyncWorkTreeToHead_DoesNotClobberBeadsArchiveJSONL` in `execute_bead_land_test.go`
- **AC4-11**: All nine queue-rank lifecycle tests in `cli/internal/bead/queue_lifecycle_test.go`
- **AC12**: `TestQueueRank_SetFlagStringRoundTrip` verifies the `--set queue-rank=5` string round-trip
- **AC13**: Full test suite green (`./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/...`)
- **AC14**: `lefthook run pre-commit` passes
